2606.13571.title: Existence Precedes Value: Joint Modeling of Observational Existence and Evolving States in Time Series Forecasting
2606.13571.paper_summary: Real-world time series are often highly incomplete and irregular due to sensor dormancy, transmission delays, and event-driven sampling, making reliable forecasting fundamentally challenging. Existing methods have evolved from impute-then-forecast pipelines to continuous-time models such as Neural ODEs and continuous-time graph networks. While these approaches improve the modeling of historical irregularity, they still rely on an implicit oracle assumption at inference time: the timestamps of future valid observations are presumed to be known in advance. This assumption limits practical relevance, since in many real systems the more fundamental question is not only what the future value will be, but also whether a valid observation will occur at all. In this paper, we propose Timeflies, a unified framework that reformulates forecasting as a joint problem of future observability inference and value estimation. To explicitly model the interaction between observation dynamics and state evolution, Timeflies adopts an observation stream and a value stream, coupled through three dedicated modules for reliability-aware embedding, observation-guided dependency modeling, and joint prediction. We further construct Shadow, a benchmark that combines natural missingness from public datasets with real-world industrial data, and introduce the Observation-Value Joint Entropy (OVJE) metric to comprehensively evaluate this coupled predictability. Extensive experiments show that Timeflies consistently outperforms existing methods, highlighting the importance of explicitly modeling future observability in time series forecasting with missing values. Code and dataset are available in https://github.com/ant-intl/Timeflies.
2606.13571.paper_summary_zh: 真實世界的時間序列通常因為感測器休眠、傳輸延遲和事件驅動的取樣而高度不完整和不規則，這使得可靠的預測基本上具有挑戰性。現有的方法已經從插補再預測的流程演變為像神經常微分方程（Neural ODEs）和連續時間圖網絡這樣的連續時間模型。雖然這些方法改善了對歷史不規則性的建模，但它們在推斷時仍然依賴於一個隱含的神諭假設：未來有效觀測的時間戳被假設為事先已知。這一假設限制了實際的相關性，因為在許多真實系統中，更根本的問題不僅是未來的值將是什麼，還包括是否會發生有效的觀測。在本文中，我們提出了Timeflies，一個統一的框架，將預測重新定義為未來可觀測性推斷和價值估計的聯合問題。為了明確建模觀測動態和狀態演變之間的互動，Timeflies採用了觀測流和價值流，通過三個專用模塊進行耦合，這些模塊分別用於可靠性感知嵌入、觀測引導的依賴建模和聯合預測。我們進一步構建了Shadow，一個基準，將公共數據集中的自然缺失性與真實世界的工業數據結合起來，並引入了觀測-價值聯合熵（OVJE）指標，以全面評估這種耦合的可預測性。大量實驗表明，Timeflies始終優於現有方法，突顯了在缺失值的時間序列預測中明確建模未來可觀測性的重要性。代碼和數據集可在 https://github.com/ant-intl/Timeflies 獲得。
2606.13571.author: Yifan Hu et.al.
2606.13571.authors: Yifan Hu, Hongzhou Chen, Peiyuan Liu, Yiding Liu, Zewei Dong, Jiang-Ming Yang
